text.title: Structured tool error envelope
text.summary: Every tool error is a JSON object with code, message, recoveryHint, and traceId — never a raw stack trace.

section.title: The envelope
section.p: The model reads tool errors verbatim and uses them to plan the next call. A raw exception leaks Python noise, dumps line numbers, and gives the agent no policy signal. A structured envelope gives the loop a single field (`recoveryHint`) it can branch on without re-reading the message.

rule.statement: Every tool error response MUST be a JSON object with these required fields: `code` (UPPER_SNAKE machine code), `message` (one human sentence, no stack trace), `recoveryHint` (one of `RETRY_LATER | CHECK_INPUT | TRY_ALTERNATIVE | REPORT_TO_USER | NEEDS_AUTH`), `traceId` (correlation ID). Optional: `retry_after_seconds`, `details` (sanitised, no stack frames).
rule.rationale: SHIELDA and Kumaran's MCP error-design study measured a 30%+ drop in dead-end retry loops when tools emit a closed `recoveryHint` enum vs free-form messages. The closed enum is what makes the policy mapping deterministic.

example.note: Closed code, one-sentence message, actionable hint, retry budget. Agent loop sleeps 60s and retries. Human reading logs has the traceId.

example.code: { "error": "TypeError: NoneType has no attribute 'json' at /app/tools/github.py:42\n File ..." }
example.note: Raw stack trace. Agent cannot tell if it is rate-limited, broken, or its fault. Likely retries blindly or hallucinates a fix.

antipattern.description: Returning HTTP status code AS the error code (`code: "429"`).
antipattern.reason: Status codes describe transport, not the agent action. Use semantic codes (`UPSTREAM_RATE_LIMITED`, `INVALID_ARGUMENT`) and let the hint enum drive the loop.

antipattern.description: Stuffing the upstream stack trace into `details`.
antipattern.reason: Leaks implementation, fills context, and gives the model no actionable signal. Sanitise: keep what the agent can act on, drop the rest.

section.reference: Closed enum of recovery hints with one-line semantics.
Kumaran ISK — LLM-friendly error handling for MCP.
